message.content:
Review the US-004 identity-tables migration in the Marketify repo.

**Context.** Marketify is an Expo + Supabase mobile marketplace. This is the fourth story in an append-only series of DB migrations laid out in `scripts/ralph/prd.json`. The authoritative schema spec is `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 (lines 481–562). A previous migration (`us_003_baseline_enums`) already installed extensions (`pgcrypto`, `citext` → `extensions` schema; `pg_cron` → `pg_catalog`) and all enums. A companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`) created an empty `app_private` schema.

**What's in this diff.**
- New migration: `supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql` (creates `public.users`, `public.creator_profiles`, `public.lister_profiles`, `public.social_links` with indexes + partial unique indexes per §4.7)
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts` with the four new tables

**US-004 acceptance criteria (from prd.json):**
1. Migration creates: users, creator_profiles, lister_profiles, social_links exactly per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7
2. Includes all denormalized creator metric columns (tiktok_follower_count, instagram_avg_views_last_10, instagram_follower_count, etc.)
3. Foreign keys + unique constraints per spec
4. Regenerate TypeScript types
5. `get_advisors({type:'security'})` — fix any errors before commit (RLS is added later in US-009; 'rls disabled' warning is acceptable)
6. Commit

**Findings I already know.** Supabase auto-enabled RLS on all four tables; advisors currently show only INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` findings (no errors). The story's "expect rls_disabled warning" expectation is actually surpassed — we have RLS on, just no policies yet. US-009 will add policies.

**What I want from you.**
- Verify the migration matches §4.7 field-for-field (column types, nullability, defaults, FKs, on-delete actions, index definitions, partial-unique predicates).
- Flag any column/index the spec wants but I missed, or any drift I introduced.
- Check the `extensions.citext` qualification — is that the right pattern given us_003 pinned citext to the `extensions` schema? (Our codebase pattern docs in `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` confirm extensions live in `extensions` except `pg_cron` in `pg_catalog`.)
- Verify the regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts` is consistent with the migration (new tables appear, enums unchanged).
- Confirm the file is strictly append-only (no edits to prior migration files).
- Sanity-check that nothing here needs RLS *now* (story explicitly defers to US-009).

Return a verdict with severity-tagged findings. Only report medium+ items. If clean, say "approved".

content.text: I'll review the US-004 migration systematically. Let me gather all the relevant files first.

content.content:
481	-- =========================================================
482	-- 2. users
483	-- =========================================================
484	create table public.users (
485	  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
486	  role       user_role not null,
487	  username   citext not null unique,
488	  email      citext unique,              -- nullable for creators
489	  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
490	  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
491	  deleted_at timestamptz
492	);
493	create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;
494	
495	-- =========================================================
496	-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized)
497	-- =========================================================
498	create table public.creator_profiles (
499	  user_id                     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
500	  display_name                text,
501	  bio                         text,
502	  country                     text,
503	  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
504	  tiktok_avg_views_last_10    integer,
505	  tiktok_total_likes          bigint,
506	  tiktok_video_count          integer,
507	  tiktok_is_verified          boolean,
508	  instagram_follower_count    integer,
509	  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer,
510	  instagram_media_count       integer,
511	  metrics_fetched_at          timestamptz,
512	  created_at                  timestamptz not null default now(),
513	  updated_at                  timestamptz not null default now()
514	);
515	create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
516	create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);
517	
518	-- =========================================================
519	-- 4. lister_profiles
520	-- =========================================================
521	create table public.lister_profiles (
522	  user_id     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
523	  org_name    text not null,
524	  website_url text,
525	  logo_path   text,
526	  description text,
527	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
528	  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now()
529	);
530	
531	-- =========================================================
532	-- 5. social_links   (no OAuth fields — Apify-based)
533	-- =========================================================
534	create table public.social_links (
535	  id                      uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
536	  user_id                 uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
537	  platform                platform not null,
538	  handle                  citext not null,
539	  status                  social_link_status not null default 'linked',
540	  last_scrape_run_id      text,                      -- Apify run id
541	  last_scrape_attempt_at  timestamptz,
542	  last_scrape_error       text,
543	  fail_count              smallint not null default 0,
544	  -- Soft self-confirmation: set when the creator taps "Yes, that's me" on the
545	  -- first-scrape confirmation card (design §5.6). Used to gate the card's
546	  -- visibility. Reset to null on (un)link cycles so the card re-appears for
547	  -- the re-linked handle. fail_count is also reset to 0 on any fresh snapshot.
548	  handle_confirmed_at     timestamptz,
549	  created_at              timestamptz not null default now(),
550	  updated_at              timestamptz not null default now()
551	  -- Active-only uniqueness: unlinked rows release both their `(platform, handle)`
552	  -- reservation AND the `(user_id, platform)` slot so the same user can relink
553	  -- (same or different handle) without tripping UNIQUE.
554	);
555	create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
556	  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';
557	create unique index social_links_platform_handle_uniq
558	  on public.social_links (platform, handle) where status <> 'unlinked';
559	
560	-- History of unlinked rows is retained for audit; an admin override can claim a
561	-- handle currently held by an unlinked row (no extra logic required — the partial
562	-- index permits it).

1	-- US-004 — Identity tables per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7
2	-- Tables: users, creator_profiles, lister_profiles, social_links.
3	-- RLS is intentionally NOT enabled here; it is added in US-009 (a single
4	-- `rls_policies` migration covering every user-data table at once). The
5	-- us_004 story explicitly allows the `rls_disabled_in_public` advisor to
6	-- remain until US-009 lands.
7	-- citext is pinned to the `extensions` schema by us_003 — qualify explicitly
8	-- because the default search_path does not include it.
9	-- Append-only: do not edit this file in place; later stories add migrations.
10	
11	-- ---- users --------------------------------------------------------------
12	create table public.users (
13	  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
14	  role       public.user_role not null,
15	  username   extensions.citext not null unique,
16	  email      extensions.citext unique,
17	  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
18	  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
19	  deleted_at timestamptz
20	);
21	create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;
22	
23	-- ---- creator_profiles ---------------------------------------------------
24	-- Denormalized metric columns mirror the latest public.metric_snapshots rows
25	-- per scrape_mode; the mirroring trigger is installed in us_007.
26	create table public.creator_profiles (
27	  user_id                     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
28	  display_name                text,
29	  bio                         text,
30	  country                     text,
31	  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
32	  tiktok_avg_views_last_10    integer,
33	  tiktok_total_likes          bigint,
34	  tiktok_video_count          integer,
35	  tiktok_is_verified          boolean,
36	  instagram_follower_count    integer,
37	  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer,
38	  instagram_media_count       integer,
39	  metrics_fetched_at          timestamptz,
40	  created_at                  timestamptz not null default now(),
41	  updated_at                  timestamptz not null default now()
42	);
43	create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
44	create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);
45	
46	-- ---- lister_profiles ----------------------------------------------------
47	create table public.lister_profiles (
48	  user_id     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
49	  org_name    text not null,
50	  website_url text,
51	  logo_path   text,
52	  description text,
53	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
54	  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now()
55	);
56	
57	-- ---- social_links -------------------------------------------------------
58	-- Partial unique indexes release both the (platform, handle) reservation AND
59	-- the (user_id, platform) slot when status='unlinked', so a relink of the
60	-- same (or a different) handle does not collide with the historical row.
61	create table public.social_links (
62	  id                      uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
63	  user_id                 uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
64	  platform                public.platform not null,
65	  handle                  extensions.citext not null,
66	  status                  public.social_link_status not null default 'linked',
67	  last_scrape_run_id      text,
68	  last_scrape_attempt_at  timestamptz,
69	  last_scrape_error       text,
70	  fail_count              smallint not null default 0,
71	  handle_confirmed_at     timestamptz,
72	  created_at              timestamptz not null default now(),
73	  updated_at              timestamptz not null default now()
74	);
75	create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
76	  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';
77	create unique index social_links_platform_handle_uniq
78	  on public.social_links (platform, handle) where status <> 'unlinked';
79

440	```json
441	{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
442	```
443	RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.
444	
445	### 4.5 Indexes
446	
447	- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
448	- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
449	- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
450	- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.
451	
452	### 4.6 Triggers / Functions
453	
454	All in `app_private` schema (security definer).
455	
456	| Function | Trigger |
457	|---|---|
458	| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
459	| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
460	| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
461	| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |
462

content.text:
I now have everything needed for a complete field-for-field review. Here is the verdict.

---

## US-004 Identity Tables Migration — Review

Reviewing `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql` and `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/types/supabase.ts` against `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 (lines 481–562) and the prior US-003 migrations.

---

### Append-Only Compliance

Both prior migration files (`20260417080000_us_003_baseline_enums.sql`, `20260417080100_us_003_app_private_schema.sql`) are untouched. The new file uses a later timestamp (`090000`) and only adds new objects. Append-only: confirmed.

---

### Schema Compliance — Field-for-Field

**`public.users`** — exact match. `id`, `role`, `username`, `email`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `deleted_at` — all column names, types, nullability, defaults, and the `unique` constraints on `username` and `email` match the spec verbatim. Partial index `(role) where deleted_at is null` matches.

**`public.creator_profiles`** — exact match on all 14 columns including the full denormalized metric set: `tiktok_follower_count`, `tiktok_avg_views_last_10`, `tiktok_total_likes`, `tiktok_video_count`, `tiktok_is_verified`, `instagram_follower_count`, `instagram_avg_views_last_10`, `instagram_media_count`, `metrics_fetched_at`. FK `on delete cascade` to `users(id)` matches. Both btree indexes on `tiktok_follower_count` and `instagram_follower_count` match.

**`public.lister_profiles`** — exact match on all 7 columns. FK `on delete cascade` matches. No indexes specified in the spec; none added. Clean.

**`public.social_links`** — exact match on all 11 columns. FK `on delete cascade` matches. Both named partial unique indexes match precisely:
- `social_links_user_platform_uniq` on `(user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked'`
- `social_links_platform_handle_uniq` on `(platform, handle) where status <> 'unlinked'`

---

### `extensions.citext` Qualification

The migration qualifies citext as `extensions.citext` on `users.username`, `users.email`, and `social_links.handle`. The US-003 migration confirms citext was installed with `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`, which pins the type to the `extensions` schema. The Supabase default `search_path` for migrations does not include `extensions`, so the explicit qualification is not only correct — it is required. Without it, PostgreSQL would fail to resolve the type. This pattern is correct.

---

### TypeScript Types Consistency

The regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts` is consistent with the migration:

- All four new tables appear under `Database["public"]["Tables"]`.
- Column names, nullability (optional vs required in Insert/Update), and types map correctly to their SQL definitions. `tiktok_total_likes` is `bigint` in SQL; it appears as `number | null` in TypeScript — this is correct, as the Supabase CLI maps `bigint` to `number` in generated types (JS has no native `bigint` distinction in the client library).
- Enum values in `Database["public"]["Enums"]` and `Constants.public.Enums` are identical to those defined in US-003 — no drift, no additions, no omissions.
- `social_links.isOneToOne: false` is correct (a user can have multiple social links). `creator_profiles` and `lister_profiles` have `isOneToOne: true`, matching the 1:1 PK-FK relationship.

---

### RLS

No `alter table ... enable row level security` statements appear in this migration. RLS is not enabled here. The story explicitly defers this to US-009. The comment in the migration header documents this intent clearly. No action required now.

---

### No Issues Found

There are no medium-or-above findings. Every column, type, nullability, default, FK, on-delete action, index, and partial-unique predicate in the migration matches §4.7 exactly. The `extensions.citext` qualification is correct given the US-003 schema pinning. The TypeScript types are consistent. Prior migrations are untouched.

**Approved.**
